info.name: Enterprise ERP Collection
info.description:
Multi-tenant ERP API.

Tenant identification is header-based: every tenant-scoped request sends `tenant: {{tenant_id}}` (added automatically by the collection-level pre-request script). No subdomain required — all requests hit `{{base_url}}` (default `http://localhost:8000`).

Bootstrap order on a clean DB:
1. **Onboard Tenant** (central, no tenant header) → captures `tenant_id`.
2. (shell) `php artisan tenants:seed` — seeds permissions, super-admin user, workflow statuses, default leave types.
3. **Login** → captures `token` and `user_id`.
4. Any tenant-scoped request below.

Default seeded credentials: `admin@erp.local / Admin@1234!` (super-admin), `staff@erp.local / Staff@1234!` (staff).

item.name: Central Routing
item.description: Endpoints served by the central host (no tenant context). No `tenant` header required — the collection-level pre-request script still sends one but the central middleware ignores it on `/api/tenants`.

request.description: Creates a tenant row + tenant database (CreateDatabase + MigrateDatabase pipeline runs synchronously). You MUST then run `php artisan tenants:seed` for that tenant before logging in.

item.name: Tenant API
item.description: All requests below hit `{{base_url}}` and rely on the `tenant: {{tenant_id}}` header (added by the collection-level pre-request script) for tenant resolution via Stancl's `InitializeTenancyByRequestData` middleware.

exec: pm.test('Login succeeded and token captured', function () {

request.description: Authenticates against the tenant's user table and issues a Passport personal-access token.
Revokes the current Passport access token.

Generates an RFC 6238 TOTP secret on the user and returns the `otpauth://` provisioning URI. `mfa_enabled` stays false until `Verify MFA` succeeds. Test script captures the base32 secret into `{{mfa_secret}}`.
Validates the 6-digit code against the stored secret (±1 time-step window). On first success, sets `mfa_enabled = true`.
Lists tenant roles eager-loaded with permissions and parent role. Captures the first role's UUID into `{{role_id}}`.
Returns the role plus `effective_permissions` (walked through the parent chain, cycle-safe).
`parent_role_id` is optional. Set to another role UUID to inherit its permissions (self-reference and cycles are rejected with 422).
Lists the tenant's permission catalog (IAM + HRM bundles seeded by `tenants:seed`). Captures the first three UUIDs into `{{permission_ids}}` for `Sync Role Permissions`.

item.name: Workflow Statuses
item.description: Tenant-scoped lifecycle status registry for every state-machine module (`hrm.application`, `hrm.leave`, `hrm.appraisal`, `hrm.vacancy`, `hrm.employee`, `hrm.payroll_period`). Seeded with defaults; admins can rename/reorder/recolor/add.

request.description: `module` query is optional — omit to get every module's statuses.
Unauthenticated. Returns name + protocol of active providers for the tenant — used by the login page to render SSO buttons.
`client_secret` is stored encrypted (Eloquent `encrypted` cast). SAML protocol is reserved but rejected with 422 by the redirect endpoint.
Returns the IdP authorization URL. The state token is cached server-side (10 min) — your browser is then expected to redirect the user to that URL.
Exchanges the IdP authorization code for an ID token, resolves or JIT-provisions the local user, and returns a Passport access token.
Updates tenant branding columns (`logo_path`, `primary_color`, `secondary_color`). Migration `2026_05_21_100000_add_branding_columns_to_tenants_table` must be applied.
Paginated (50 per page) audit entries written by the Auditable trait. Attached to Role, Permission, AuditLog (no-op), WorkflowStatus, SsoProvider, and all HRM models.

item.name: HRM Module
item.description: Human Resource Management — Workforce, Leave, Payroll, Recruitment, Performance, Suggestions, Notes & Documents. All endpoints require the `tenant` header (set automatically) and a valid Passport token (`Login` first).

request.description: Paginated. Filters: `q` (name/code), `include_inactive=1`.

Sensitive PII (`national_id`, `bank_account`, `tax_id`) and `base_salary` are hidden on the model's `$hidden` array and never serialized — but they ARE returned encrypted in the DB. Salaries are encrypted at rest using `APP_KEY`.
`employee_id` is the human-readable identifier (`TT-NNNN`). Auto-generated by `RecruitmentService::generateNextEmployeeId()` when created via the application convert flow.
Validates the `hrm.employee` workflow transition to `terminated`, stamps `termination_date`, and soft-deletes (releases the email from the partial unique index).
Reactivates a soft-deleted employee. 422 if the row isn't trashed.
Locks the corresponding `leave_balances` row (`FOR UPDATE`) and rejects with 422 if available balance is insufficient. `days` is auto-calculated from the date range when omitted.
Caller must have a linked Employee row (`employees.user_id = caller.user_id`). Decrements `pending`, increments `used`. 422 if caller is not linked.
Idempotent. Computes a Payslip for every active employee, applying global pay components + any employee-specific overrides effective in the period. Sets period status to `closed`.

body.raw:
{
    "title": "Senior Backend Engineer",
    "reference": "VAC-2026-0001",
    "department_id": "{{department_id}}",
    "position_id": "{{position_id}}",
    "description": "Build the ERP backend...",
    "requirements": "Laravel, PostgreSQL, multi-tenant experience",
    "location": "Remote",
    "salary_min": 60000,
    "salary_max": 120000,
    "employment_type": "full_time",
    "opens_at": "2026-05-22",
    "closes_at": "2026-06-30"
}

request.description: Status transitions are validated against `hrm.vacancy` workflow (draft → open → closed/filled).

Moves the application along the `hrm.application` state machine. To reach `hired` from `applied`, you may need to step through `screening` → `interview` → `offer` → `hired` depending on the configured `allowed_transitions`.

Application must be `hired`. Idempotent: returns the same employee on repeat. Dedupe-by-email: an active employee with the same email is linked rather than cloned (`linkedExisting: true`). Auto-issues a `TT-NNNN` employee_id.
1–200 UUIDs. Returns `{ converted, alreadyLinked, ineligible, missing, errors }`.
Allowed within `RecruitmentService::REVERT_CONVERSION_WINDOW_DAYS` (= 7 days) of conversion. Renames the employee_id to `<original>-REV-<rand>` (freeing the original for re-issue) and soft-deletes.

body.raw:
{
    "rating": 4,
    "recommendation": "hire",
    "strengths": "Strong Laravel fundamentals.",
    "weaknesses": "Limited Postgres tuning experience.",
    "notes": "Recommend for offer."
}

request.description: When `is_anonymous = true`, the response strips `employee_id` and the submitter relationship — admins can't derive identity from the JSON.
`action` ∈ `acknowledge | action | dismiss`. State machine: new → acknowledged → actioned, with `dismiss` allowed from new or acknowledged.

body.raw:
{
    "employee_id": "{{employee_uuid}}",
    "category": "performance",
    "title": "Q2 1:1 notes",
    "body": "Discussed onboarding feedback and growth path.",
    "is_private": true,
    "is_disciplinary": false,
    "incident_date": "2026-05-21"
}

request.description: `expiring_soon=1` filters to documents expiring within 30 days.

Stores metadata only — the actual file should already be uploaded to tenant-scoped storage (`tenant_path(...)`). Add file-upload middleware separately if you want this endpoint to accept multipart bodies.
